_doc: PMF roadmap seed. Phase 1 = full pre-PMF → 40% journey (Idee → MVP → Erste User → Survey → Iteration). Other phases unchanged. INVARIANT: here:true on exactly ONE sub. Default start = 1.1; onboarding routing may set 1.3/1.4/1.5 if user already past those steps.

subs.name: Problem & Founder-Fit
subs.question: Bin ich der richtige Founder für ein echtes Problem?
subs.exit: Validiertes Problem mit ≥5 unabhängigen User-Quotes; klare Solution-Hypothese; Founder-Market-Fit artikuliert.

subs.name: MVP scope & build
subs.question: Was ist die kleinste Version, die das Problem löst?
deliverables: MVP live (manual / hacky / fake-it-with-Google-Forms ist OK)
subs.exit: MVP live; ≥3 echte User nutzen es ohne Founder-Hand-Holding.

subs.name: Erste 10 User & Lernen
subs.question: Kann ich 10–30 User manuell rekrutieren und von ihnen lernen?
deliverables: Session-Notes pro User (was probiert, wo gestockt)
subs.exit: ≥10 engagierte User aktiv; ≥3 Friktion-Patterns aus Beobachtung (nicht aus Selbstauskunft).

subs.name: PMF-Score messen & Lieblings-User identifizieren
subs.question: Wie hoch ist mein PMF-Score und wer ist mein idealer Kunde?
deliverables: Survey-Link nur an engaged users (Manual-Follow-up bei Non-Respondern)
Lieblings-User-Persona v1 (extrahiert aus Very-Disappointed, 5 Pflichtfelder: Name, Rolle, Kontext, Hauptnutzen, Frustration)
subs.exit: Score berechnet (≥15 directional / ≥40 benchmark-valid); Lieblings-User v1 mit 5 Pflichtfeldern; Segmentation-Sheet komplett.

subs.name: Verbessern bis User dein Produkt nicht mehr missen wollen
subs.question: Habe ich echtes Product/Market Fit erreicht?

phases.name: Search for Go-to-Market Fit
phases.gate: Locked until Sean Ellis Score ≥ 40% (confirmed in 2 consecutive monthly reviews)

subs.name: Find repeatable sales motion
subs.question: Will many people pay?
subs.exit: The same ICP, pain, message, and sales motion repeatedly create paying customers.

subs.name: Prove non-founders can sell
subs.question: Can non-founders sell this?
subs.exit: A non-founder can close customers without the founder driving every step.

subs.name: Make it scalable
subs.question: Does this scale?
subs.exit: The sales motion keeps working as volume increases without creating chaos.

subs.name: Ensure customer success
subs.question: Can we scale without churn?
subs.exit: New customers consistently reach value, stay active, and do not churn faster as growth increases.

subs.name: Make it profitable
subs.question: Can we scale profitably?
subs.exit: Growth produces profitable revenue with acceptable acquisition cost, payback, and delivery margin.

phases.name: Scaling the business
phases.gate: Locked until repeatable + profitable sales motion proven.

subs.name: Hit the gas & scale
subs.question: Does this keep scaling when we hit the gas?
subs.exit: Increasing resources creates more growth without breaking the sales, product, or delivery system.

subs.name: Scale the organization & processes
subs.question: Can the organization handle the growth?
subs.exit: The business can operate predictably without every important decision flowing through the founder.

subs.name: Expand internationally
subs.question: Can this work in new markets?
subs.exit: At least one new market shows early repeatability without requiring a completely new business model.

subs.name: Add additional product lines
subs.question: Can we expand the product without losing focus?
subs.exit: A new product line creates additional revenue from the existing customer base without weakening the core business.
